data.title: Create promptfoo eval config for prior-art search trigger accuracy
data.description:
GREEN: Create the promptfoo eval suite that verifies prior-art search bright-line triggers.

File: plugins/dso/skills/shared/evals/promptfooconfig.yaml
Pattern reference: plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/evals/promptfooconfig.yaml

Content requirements (from story done definitions):

1. Boundary scenarios (>= 3 tests): Scenarios where agent must decide search vs no-search
   - New utility being created (should trigger search)
   - External API integration (should trigger search)
   - Recurrence of known pattern with open bug (should trigger search — hard blocker)
   
2. False-positive exclusion scenarios (>= 3 tests): Routine modifications that must NOT trigger
   - Single-file logic fix within existing pattern (no search)
   - Formatting/lint-only change (no search)
   - Test reversion to known-good state (no search)

3. Incident replay scenarios (>= 3 tests): Recent confident-ignorance incidents
   - ACLI priority gap (unfamiliar CLI tool, should have searched GitHub for usage patterns)
   - Bash syntax cascading failures (unfamiliar shell pattern, should have searched)
   - Third incident from project history (identify from epic description/comments)

Use haiku as default grader model. Use llm-rubric assertions checking for consistent search/no-search classification.

Also remove RED marker from .test-index.

File Impact:
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/evals/promptfooconfig.yaml (create)
- .test-index (edit — remove RED marker)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All eval structure tests pass (GREEN) - Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-prior-art-search-eval.sh
- [ ] Test gate exits 0 - Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] Eval config exists and non-empty - Verify: test -s plugins/dso/skills/shared/evals/promptfooconfig.yaml
- [ ] RED marker removed - Verify: ! grep -q 'shared/evals/promptfooconfig.yaml.*\[test_eval_config_exists\]' .test-index
- [ ] Eval discoverable by runner - Verify: plugins/dso/scripts/run-skill-evals.sh --all 2>&1 | grep -q shared
